reading_order.purpose: This file defines the data model, tests, semantic definitions, and metrics for order items in a food service business. It enables analysis of revenue, costs, and profitability at the granularity of individual order items.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining order, product, and supply cost data to enable detailed analysis of order profitability and item characteristics. It aggregates supply costs per product and enriches order items with product details and ordering timestamps.
reading_order.reason: architectural hub; core business logic; analytical output

reading_order.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with item-level summaries and customer ordering patterns. It aggregates order items to calculate costs, subtotals, and item counts while determining food/drink order types and customer order sequence.
reading_order.reason: architectural hub; core business logic; analytical output

reading_order.purpose: This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing business logic for calculating order totals, categorizing orders by food/drink items, and defining metrics for order analysis. It serves as the canonical source for order-related business intelligence.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: This model creates a customer-level summary table that enriches customer data with order history metrics including lifetime spend, order frequency, and customer type classification. It serves as a foundational dataset for customer analytics and segmentation.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates lifetime order and spending metrics per customer, supporting customer analytics and segmentation. It also exposes these metrics through semantic models and predefined queries for reporting.

reading_order.purpose: Defines the raw data sources and table schemas for an e-commerce system, specifically configuring metadata for customer, order, item, store, product, and supply data from the Jaffle Shop business.

reading_order.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields while adding calculated columns to classify products as food or drink items.

reading_order.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data by renaming columns to establish consistent naming conventions for downstream consumption. It extracts customer identifiers and names from the e-commerce source system to create a standardized customer dataset.
This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format for downstream analytics use. It cleans and renames store-related fields while extracting date information from timestamps.

This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data by renaming and selecting key fields to create a standardized view of order items. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw data ingestion and downstream analytics or modeling.

This staging model transforms raw supply data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields while adding calculated columns like supply UUID and converted cost values.

This is a task automation configuration file that orchestrates data generation and loading workflows for analytics projects. It defines commands for setting up virtual environments, installing dependencies, generating synthetic data, and loading it into a database.

This macro provides a standardized way to convert cent values to dollar amounts across different database platforms, ensuring consistent decimal precision and rounding behavior. It abstracts database-specific syntax for numeric conversion operations.

reading_order.reason: core business logic; shared utility; entry point

reading_order.purpose: This model generates a time spine table containing a continuous sequence of dates, typically used for time-series analysis and metric calculations in downstream reporting. It creates a foundational date dimension spanning approximately 10 years (3650 days) starting from a base date.

This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks that automatically validate and format code before commits are made, ensuring code quality standards are maintained. It specifies checks for YAML syntax, file formatting, and Python code style enforcement.

This is the main configuration file for a dbt project named 'jaffle_shop' that defines project metadata, file paths, and model configurations. It serves as the central configuration hub that controls how dbt builds and deploys data models.

This semantic model defines the product dimension for analytics, providing a structured representation of product data with entities and dimensions that can be used to contextualize metrics. It serves as a dimensional model for product-related business intelligence reporting.

reading_order.purpose: This model creates a final products mart by selecting all data from the staging products table. It serves as a clean, ready-to-use dataset for downstream reporting and analysis on product information.

This model creates a supplies mart by selecting all data from the staging supplies table. It serves as a direct pass-through layer that makes supplies data available for downstream consumption in the analytics environment.

This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for cleaned customer data, ensuring each record represents a unique customer with proper identification. It serves as a standardized intermediate layer between raw customer data and final analytical models.

This staging model defines the structure and validation for cleaned location data, ensuring each location has a unique ID and non-null values, while also including unit tests to verify proper date truncation logic for store opening times.
This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for individual food and drink items that comprise customer orders, ensuring data quality through defined constraints and relationships.

This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for cleaned order data, ensuring each order has a unique identifier and valid financial calculations. It serves as a standardized intermediate layer for order information in the data warehouse.

This YAML file defines the structure and validation rules for a staging layer product data model. It specifies the schema for cleaned and transformed product information with uniqueness and non-null constraints on the primary key.
This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for supply expense data, ensuring data quality through uniqueness and non-null constraints on supply identifiers.

This semantic model defines the analytical structure for location data, establishing how location dimensions and measures should be interpreted and aggregated in business intelligence contexts. It serves as a metadata layer that maps the physical locations table to analytical concepts like tax rates and operational dates.

This model serves as a pass-through layer that exposes all location data from the staging layer for downstream consumption. It provides a clean interface for accessing standardized location information without additional transformation.
